seo.title: Missouri Dental Assistant License (2026) | Requirements & Fees
seo.description: Complete guide to dental assistant licensing in Missouri — endorsement steps, fees, DANB certification, and radiography permits.
seo.keywords: dental assistant license missouri, dental assistant certification missouri, RDA missouri, DANB CDA missouri, dental assistant radiography missouri

board.name: Missouri Dental Board
board.mailing_address:
Missouri Dental Board
3605 Missouri Blvd.
P.O. Box 1367
Jefferson City, MO 65102

quick_facts.ce_requirement: 2 hours of continuing education per renewal cycle related to infection control or safety.

license_types.title: Expanded Functions Dental Assistant
license_types.description: The primary credential for dental assistants in Missouri.

steps.title: Understand Missouri's Requirements
steps.description: Confirm that Missouri does not require general dental assistant licensure. If seeking qualification for advanced functions (such as Nitrous Oxide Monitoring, Restorative I/II, or Local Anesthesia Administration), ensure you meet specific educational, examination, and/or experience criteria outlined by the Missouri Dental Board.

steps.title: Download Application Form
steps.description: Navigate to the Missouri Dental Board Application Forms page (https://scm.dpr.mo.gov/dental/apply.asp) and download the specific PDF application form corresponding to the advanced function qualification you are seeking.

steps.title: Complete Application and Gather Documents
steps.description: Fill out the downloaded PDF application thoroughly. Collect all necessary supporting documents, which typically include proof of approved course completion, examination results, and a current Basic Life Support (BLS) for healthcare providers certification.

steps.title: Prepare Application Fee
steps.description: Include a check or money order, made payable to the Missouri Dental Board, for the non-refundable application fee (e.g., $30.00 for Nitrous Oxide Monitoring or Restorative I/II).

steps.title: Submit Application Packet
steps.description: Mail the completed application form, all original or certified copies of supporting documentation, and the application fee to the Missouri Dental Board's mailing address for processing.

documents.detail: Certificate from CODA-accredited program or documentation of on-the-job training hours.
Official CDA certification from the Dental Assisting National Board, if applicable.
Proof of radiography competency/certification required for taking x-rays.

provisional_text: Some states allow dental assistants to work under direct supervision while obtaining required certifications or completing training.
reciprocity_text: Dental assistants can apply for Expanded Functions Dental Assistant through the Missouri Dental Board by registration.

faqs.question: Does Missouri require a dental assistant license?
faqs.answer: Yes, Missouri requires Expanded Functions Dental Assistant through the state board.

faqs.question: Is DANB certification recognized in Missouri?
faqs.answer: Yes, DANB CDA certification is recognized and often preferred by employers in Missouri.

faqs.question: Can I take dental x-rays in Missouri?
faqs.answer: Yes, but a radiography permit or certification is required.

faqs.question: How much does it cost to become a dental assistant in Missouri?
faqs.answer: State application fees are $10. Additional costs may include DANB CDA exam ($450), radiography certification, and background check fees.

reciprocity.notes: General dental assistants are not licensed. Expanded functions permit is $10.

hero.title: Become a Dental Assistant in Missouri
hero.dek: To obtain qualification for advanced dental assistant functions (e.g., Nitrous Oxide Monitoring, Restorative I/II, Local Anesthesia Administration) in Missouri, you must download, complete, and submit the relevant PDF application form from the Missouri Dental Board Application Forms page.

official_links.registration_steps: Missouri does not require general licensure for Dental Assistants. For specific advanced functions (e.g., Nitrous Oxide Monitoring, Restorative I/II, Local Anesthesia Administration), applicants must download, complete, and submit the relevant PDF application form by mail with all required supporting documentation and fees.
